texto: No matter what choice you make, it doesn't define you. Not forever. People can make bad choices and change their minds and hearts and do good things later; just as people can make good choices and then turn around and walk a bad path. No choice we make lasts our whole life. It there's ever a choice you've made that you no longer agree with, you can make another choice . . . you can't change the past. Ah, but the future . . . you own the future. So, tell me . . . what choice do you want to make now?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jonathan Mayberry que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “No matter what choice you make, it doesn't define you. Not forever. People can make bad choices a…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, choices, future, com foco em mudanca e future.
